title: Browse All Sheets
description: Essential CLI commands for browsing and managing sheets.

commands.description: List directory contents
commands.scenario: Use to view files and directories in the current directory.

commands.description: Change the current directory
commands.scenario: Navigate to a different directory to access files or run scripts.

commands.description: Print working directory
commands.scenario: Determine your current directory location in the filesystem.

commands.description: Concatenate and display file content
commands.scenario: Quickly view the content of a file in the terminal.

commands.description: Search for a term in a file
commands.scenario: Find specific information in a file by searching for keywords.

commands.description: Search for files in a directory hierarchy
commands.scenario: Locate files within a directory and its subdirectories.

commands.description: Move or rename files or directories
commands.scenario: Organize files by moving them to different directories or renaming them.

commands.description: Copy files or directories
commands.scenario: Duplicate files or directories to another location.

commands.description: Remove files or directories
commands.scenario: Delete files or directories that are no longer needed.

commands.description: Change file permissions
commands.scenario: Modify file permissions to control access rights.

commands.description: Create a compressed archive of a directory
commands.scenario: Compress a directory into a .tar.gz file for storage or transfer.

commands.description: Extract files from a ZIP archive
commands.scenario: Decompress a .zip file to access its contents.

commands.description: Display the first 10 lines of a file
commands.scenario: Preview the beginning of a file to understand its structure or content.

commands.description: Display the last 10 lines of a file
commands.scenario: View the end of a file, often used for logs to see recent entries.

commands.description: Compare two files line by line
commands.scenario: Identify differences between two files, useful for version control.

commands.description: Write text to a file
commands.scenario: Create a new file or overwrite an existing file with specific text.

commands.description: Edit a file using the nano text editor
commands.scenario: Make quick edits to a file directly from the terminal.

commands.description: Periodically runs the ls command every second to monitor real-time file changes or directory updates.
Displays the total disk usage of a specific directory in a human-readable format (e.g., MB, GB).
Provides detailed metadata about a file, including exact size, inode number, and precise timestamps for access, modification, and change.
Lists all open files and the processes that are currently accessing them, useful for troubleshooting 'file in use' errors.
A powerful pattern to pipe the output of a search or list command into a bulk move operation.
